evals.prompt:
I have a single CSV file `manufacturers.csv` with the following columns
and rows:

    name,description,country
    Dell,Server and storage vendor,US
    Juniper,Network equipment,US
    Arista,Network equipment,US

The schema defines `OrganizationManufacturer` with Text attributes `name`,
`description`, and `country`, and `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`. The
schema is fixed — do not propose changes to it.

Convert the CSV to Infrahub object YAML and save the emitted file(s) under
`./output_dir/` (create the directory). Do not load anything against a
server.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ containing at least one YAML file where every document carries the four envelope fields (apiVersion: infrahub.app/v1, kind: Object, spec.kind: OrganizationManufacturer, spec.data as a list of rows).
expectations: Every emitted YAML document has apiVersion infrahub.app/v1
Every emitted YAML document has kind Object
Every emitted YAML document has spec.kind set to OrganizationManufacturer
Every emitted YAML document has spec.data as a list of mapping rows

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

evals.prompt:
I have three CSVs that reference each other:

    # manufacturers.csv
    name,country
    Cisco,US
    Arista,US

    # device_types.csv
    name,manufacturer
    c9300-48,Cisco
    7050x-32,Arista

    # devices.csv
    name,device_type,site
    spine-01,7050x-32,par-1
    leaf-01,c9300-48,par-1

The schema declares OrganizationManufacturer, DcimDeviceType, DcimDevice,
and LocationSite — each with `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`. The
schema is fixed.

Emit Infrahub object YAML under `./output_dir/`. Make sure files load in
the right order against `infrahubctl object load`.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where every emitted YAML file uses an NN_ numeric prefix in its filename, and where each kind is produced by exactly one file so the load order is unambiguous.
expectations: Every emitted YAML filename starts with an NN_ numeric prefix
Manufacturers / device types / sites load before devices
Each kind is emitted by exactly one file
Envelope (apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, spec.data) is correct

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: load-order-numbering
assertions.check: Every emitted file uses an NN_ numeric prefix and each kind is produced by exactly one file

evals.prompt:
I have `devices.csv` with display-label values for a dropdown attribute:

    name,role,status
    spine-01,spine,Active
    spine-02,spine,Maintenance
    leaf-01,leaf,Active
    leaf-02,leaf,Retired

The schema's `DcimDevice.status` is a Dropdown with choices declared as:

    status:
      kind: Dropdown
      choices:
        - name: active
          label: Active
        - name: maintenance
          label: Maintenance
        - name: retired
          label: Retired

DcimDevice has `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`. The schema is fixed.

Emit Infrahub object YAML to `./output_dir/`.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where every emitted DcimDevice row sets `status` to a choice name (e.g., `active`), never to a label like `Active` — the load would otherwise be rejected with `'Active' is not a valid choice for status`.
expectations: Every status value uses a Dropdown choice `name` (active / maintenance / retired)
No status value uses a Dropdown label (Active / Maintenance / Retired)
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: dropdown-names
assertions.check: DcimDevice.status uses the choice name (active / maintenance / retired), never the display label

evals.prompt:
I have two CSV files referencing each other:

    # rooms.csv
    site_shortname,name,floor
    par,server-room-1,3
    par,server-room-2,3

    # racks.csv
    name,room,site_shortname,units
    rack-01,server-room-1,par,42
    rack-02,server-room-2,par,42

The schema declares:

    - DcimRoom with human_friendly_id: [site__shortname__value, name__value]
      (a two-element HFID)
    - DcimRack with human_friendly_id: [name__value] and a relationship
      `room` peering DcimRoom

The schema is fixed. Emit Infrahub object YAML to `./output_dir/` and make
sure the relationship references match each target's HFID arity.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where the DcimRack rows reference each room via a two-element list HFID (matching DcimRoom's HFID length), and any reference to a single-element-HFID kind (if present) is a scalar string.
expectations: DcimRack `room` reference is a two-element list HFID (matching DcimRoom)
No relationship reference is over-packed for a single-element HFID target
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: hfid-reference-shape
assertions.check: Single-element HFID targets receive scalar references; multi-element HFID targets receive a list of the correct length

evals.prompt:
I have `devices_with_interfaces.csv` where the device columns repeat
per interface:

    device_name,device_role,interface_name,interface_role,interface_status
    spine-01,spine,Ethernet1,uplink,active
    spine-01,spine,Ethernet2,uplink,active
    leaf-01,leaf,Ethernet1,uplink,active
    leaf-01,leaf,Ethernet2,server,active

The schema declares DcimDevice with a Component relationship `interfaces`
whose peer is the generic `InterfacePhysical`. The matching Parent
relationship is on the child. DcimDevice has `human_friendly_id:
[name__value]`. The schema is fixed.

Emit Infrahub object YAML to `./output_dir/`. Group interfaces under their
owning device.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where the interfaces relationship is emitted as a mapping with both `kind: InterfacePhysical` and `data: [...]`, NOT as a bare list of dicts (the loader can't pick a concrete child schema without the kind wrapper when the peer is a generic).
expectations: The `interfaces` relationship is emitted as a {kind, data} mapping
The wrapper includes `kind: InterfacePhysical` so the generic peer resolves
The wrapper's `data` is a list of interface row dicts
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: component-children-shape
assertions.check: Component children use {kind, data: [...]} wrapper mapping, not bare list of dicts

evals.prompt:
I have `interfaces.csv` for a single device, with 48 contiguous
interface names all sharing the same role and status:

    device_name,interface_name,interface_role,interface_status
    leaf-01,eth0,server,active
    leaf-01,eth1,server,active
    leaf-01,eth2,server,active
    ... (continues identically) ...
    leaf-01,eth47,server,active

The schema declares DcimDevice with a Component relationship `interfaces`
peering `InterfacePhysical`. DcimDevice has `human_friendly_id:
[name__value]`. The schema is fixed.

Emit Infrahub object YAML to `./output_dir/`. Use the loader's
range-expansion feature where appropriate — emitting 48 separate rows is
not the right shape.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where the 48 interface rows are collapsed into a single row with a bracket-range name (e.g., `eth[0-47]`) inside the interfaces children block, and where the enclosing block sets `parameters.expand_range: true` so the loader expands the range instead of creating one literal-named interface.
expectations: The 48 interface rows are collapsed to a bracket-range name (e.g., eth[0-47])
The interfaces block sets parameters.expand_range true
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: range-expansion
assertions.check: Any bracket-range child name is accompanied by parameters.expand_range: true on the enclosing relationship block

evals.prompt:
I have `devices.csv` with an extra `gpu_count` column the schema
does not declare:

    name,role,status,gpu_count
    spine-01,spine,active,0
    gpu-rig-01,gpu,active,8
    gpu-rig-02,gpu,active,8

The DcimDevice schema declares `name`, `role`, and `status` only — no
`gpu_count` attribute. The schema is fixed and I have not approved any
schema change.

Emit whatever correct Infrahub object YAML you can to `./output_dir/`. Do
NOT propose or write any schema changes; the skill fails closed on
unmapped columns.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ that contains no schema documents (no file with top-level `version: "1.0"` plus `nodes:` / `generics:`), and nothing under a `schemas/` sub-path of the output dir. The skill should either emit partial object YAML (omitting the unmapped column) with a note about the unmapped `gpu_count`, or stop emission entirely and explain — but it must never write a schema mutation.
expectations: No file in ./output_dir/ is a schema document (version 1.0 + nodes / generics)
No file lives under a schemas/ sub-path of ./output_dir/
Envelope is correct on any object YAML that is emitted

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: no-schema-mutation
assertions.check: Output dir contains no schema document and no schemas/ sub-path

evals.prompt:
I have `devices.csv` with a mix of attribute types whose values arrive as CSV strings:

    name,is_managed,gpu_count,commissioned_at,metadata
    spine-01,Yes,8,2024-01-15,"{""vlan"": 100, ""tagged"": true}"
    spine-02,No,0,2024-02-01,"{""vlan"": 200, ""tagged"": false}"

The DcimDevice schema declares `name` (Text, `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`),
`is_managed` (Boolean), `gpu_count` (Number), `commissioned_at` (DateTime), and
`metadata` (JSON). The schema is fixed.

Emit Infrahub object YAML to `./output_dir/`.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where Boolean cells emit YAML `true`/`false`, Number cells emit integers, DateTime cells emit ISO-8601 strings, and JSON cells emit parsed mappings — never the raw CSV string forms.
expectations: Boolean cells (Yes/No) emit as YAML booleans true/false
Number cells emit as YAML integers (not numeric strings)
DateTime cells emit as ISO-8601 strings
JSON cells emit as parsed YAML mappings, not encoded strings
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: value-coercion
assertions.check: Boolean / Number / DateTime / JSON attributes use native YAML types, not CSV string pass-through

evals.prompt:
I have `manufacturers.csv` with optional attributes left blank in some rows:

    name,description,country
    Dell,Server vendor,US
    Juniper,,US
    Arista,,

The OrganizationManufacturer schema declares `name` (Text,
`optional: false`, `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`), `description`
(Text, `optional: true`), and `country` (Text, `optional: true`). The
schema is fixed.

Emit Infrahub object YAML to `./output_dir/`.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where blank optional attributes are omitted entirely from the row's mapping — never emitted as `""` and never as YAML `null`.
expectations: Blank cells on optional attributes are omitted from the row mapping
No emitted attribute carries the empty string value ""
No emitted attribute carries YAML null on a fixture-tracked column
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: empty-and-null
assertions.check: Blank optional cells are absent from the row; no empty-string and no null scalars on known attributes

evals.prompt:
I have `manufacturers.csv` that was exported from a European
spreadsheet — it is semicolon-delimited and has a UTF-8 BOM at the
start. The raw content is:

    ﻿name;description;country
    Dell;Server and storage vendor;US
    Juniper;Network equipment;US
    Arista;Network equipment;US

The OrganizationManufacturer schema declares `name`, `description`,
`country` as Text attributes with `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`.
The schema is fixed.

Detect the dialect (BOM + semicolon delimiter) and emit Infrahub
object YAML to `./output_dir/`.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where every emitted string value is dialect-clean — no leftover BOM byte in the first column header, no semicolon-soup from misread delimiters (each `description` is its own attribute value, not the whole row concatenated).
expectations: The first row's name is "Dell" with no leading BOM byte
Each row's description is its own value, not "Dell;Server and storage vendor;US"
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: csv-dialect
assertions.check: Emitted string values contain no BOM byte and no semicolon-soup from misread delimiters

evals.prompt:
I have two CSVs that should produce internally consistent data:

    # manufacturers.csv
    name,country
    Cisco,US
    Arista,US

    # devices.csv
    name,manufacturer
    spine-01,Arista
    leaf-01,Cisco

The OrganizationManufacturer schema has `human_friendly_id:
[name__value]`; DcimDevice has `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`
and a `manufacturer` relationship peering OrganizationManufacturer.
The schema is fixed.

Emit Infrahub object YAML to `./output_dir/`. Make sure every device
references a manufacturer that exists in the upstream manufacturers
file — no orphan references.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where every relationship reference in devices.yml resolves to a `name` of a row in manufacturers.yml.
expectations: Every device.manufacturer value is the name of a row in the manufacturers file
No orphan references in the emission
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: pre-flight-closure
assertions.check: Every scalar relationship reference resolves to an emitted row of the target kind

evals.prompt:
I have `manufacturers.csv` with two rows:

    name,country
    Dell,US
    Juniper,US

The OrganizationManufacturer schema declares `name` (Text,
`human_friendly_id: [name__value]`) and `country` (Text). The schema
is fixed.

Emit Infrahub object YAML to `./output_dir/`. Each emitted file
must carry a leading provenance comment naming the source CSV, its
sha256, the target branch name, and the emission timestamp.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where every emitted YAML file begins with a comment block containing "Generated by infrahub-importing-data" and a "Sources:" entry naming manufacturers.csv with a sha256.
expectations: Every emitted file starts with a # Generated by infrahub-importing-data line
Every emitted file's header includes a # Sources: section
The comment block appears before the leading `---` document separator
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: provenance-comment
assertions.check: Every emitted file carries the "Generated by infrahub-importing-data" + "Sources:" provenance comment block at the top

evals.prompt:
I have `devices.csv` with these CSV headers:

    Name,Memory (GB),CPU Count,Role,Status

The schema declares `DcimDevice` with attributes:
  - name (Text)
  - memory_gb (Number)
  - cpu_count (Number)
  - role (Text)
  - status (Dropdown with choices active/maintenance/retired)

DcimDevice has `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`. There is NO
`memory_tb`, `memory_kb`, or `memory` attribute on the schema.
The schema is fixed — do not propose changes.

Sample row: `spine-01,128,32,spine,active`

Convert the CSV to Infrahub object YAML and save under `./output_dir/`.
Do not load anything against a server.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where every emitted attribute name is one of the DcimDevice schema attributes (name, memory_gb, cpu_count, role, status). Raw headers like "Memory (GB)" must have been round-tripped to snake_case (memory_gb), not retained verbatim, and no fabricated attribute names like "memory_tb" or "memory" appear.
expectations: Memory (GB) header maps to the schema attribute memory_gb
No emitted attribute name contains spaces, parentheses, or slashes
No emitted attribute is outside the schema's DcimDevice attribute list
No unit-rename artifacts (memory_tb, memory_kb) appear

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: column-to-attribute
assertions.check: Every emitted attribute name is in the schema's allowed list for its kind, with no raw-header artifacts and no unit-rename attributes

evals.prompt:
I have two CSV files that both map to the same target kind.

    # manufacturers-na.csv
    name,description,country
    Cisco,Network vendor,US
    Juniper,Network vendor,US
    Arista,Network vendor,US

    # manufacturers-eu.csv
    name,description,country
    Nokia,Network vendor,FI
    Ericsson,Network vendor,SE
    Huawei,Network vendor,CN

The schema declares `OrganizationManufacturer` with Text attributes
`name`, `description`, `country`, and `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`.
The schema is fixed.

Both inputs produce rows of the same target kind. Merge them into one
numbered output file under `./output_dir/`. Do not load anything.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ with exactly one YAML file producing OrganizationManufacturer (e.g., 01_manufacturers.yml), and its spec.data list contains rows from BOTH input CSVs (Cisco, Juniper, Arista, Nokia, Ericsson, Huawei) — six rows total, deduped by HFID.
expectations: Exactly one emitted file produces OrganizationManufacturer
The file name reflects the kind (e.g., 01_manufacturers.yml), not the inputs
The spec.data list contains at least 6 rows, covering both inputs
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: load-order-numbering
assertions.check: Each kind is produced by exactly one numbered file

assertions.name: merge-same-kind
assertions.check: OrganizationManufacturer is emitted by one file with rows from both input CSVs (>= 6 rows after dedup)

evals.prompt:
Import this CSV with lineage stamping turned on. Every value
should be stamped with `source: csv-import-20260622` using the
value+metadata mapping form.

    # manufacturers.csv
    name,description,country
    Dell,Server and storage vendor,US
    Juniper,Network equipment,US

The schema declares `OrganizationManufacturer` with Text attributes
`name`, `description`, `country`, and `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`.
The schema is fixed.

Lineage stamping is opted in: every attribute should be emitted as a
value+metadata mapping with `source: csv-import-20260622`. Assume the
Account named `csv-import-20260622` already exists; do not emit a
bootstrap file. Save the output to `./output_dir/`.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ where every attribute on every emitted row is a mapping with `value:` and `source: csv-import-20260622` — never a plain scalar — using the value+metadata form documented in the managing-objects skill.
expectations: Every attribute on every row is a mapping with value and source keys
The source value is csv-import-20260622 on every stamped attribute
No attribute is a plain scalar string
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: lineage-stamping
assertions.check: Every attribute on every emitted row uses the value+metadata mapping form with source set to csv-import-20260622

evals.prompt:
I have `devices.csv` with these columns and rows:

    name,role,status,tier_label
    spine-01,spine,Active,gold
    spine-02,spine,Maintenance,gold
    leaf-01,leaf,Active,silver

The schema declares `DcimDevice` with attributes `name` (Text),
`role` (Text), `status` (Dropdown: active/maintenance/retired). The
schema has NO `tier_label` attribute on DcimDevice. DcimDevice has
`human_friendly_id: [name__value]`. The schema is fixed.

Before emitting any file, surface every decision you need from me in
ONE batch — unmapped columns, dropdown-label translation, target
file naming, branch name, and lineage opt-in. Do not start writing
until I confirm. Echo back the full plan before any file is created.
Save planned output under `./output_dir/`.
evals.expected_output: A single batched set of multi-choice questions covering every ambiguity (tier_label has no schema home; Status is a dropdown label; branch name; lineage opt-in) plus a confirm-and-lock summary of the proposed plan. No file is written until the user confirms.
expectations: Every ambiguity is surfaced in one batched round, not one-by-one
The unmapped tier_label column is called out explicitly
Status values (Active/Maintenance) are flagged as dropdown labels
A branch name is proposed (e.g., csv-import-YYYYMMDD-HHMM)
Lineage opt-in is offered with an explanation of source vs. owner+is_protected
The plan is echoed back before any file is written

evals.prompt:
Before mapping `inventory.csv` (which I will provide
content-wise inline below), profile it: report distinct-value counts,
sample values, length ranges, numeric-only fractions, date-pattern
matches, and any sequence patterns. Use the profile to drive the
mapping plan.

    name,role,status,manufacturer_name,port_count,commissioned_at
    spine-01,spine,Active,Arista,32,2024-03-15
    spine-02,spine,Active,Arista,32,2024-03-15
    leaf-01,leaf,Maintenance,Cisco,48,2023-09-01
    leaf-02,leaf,Active,Cisco,48,2024-01-10

Schema is fixed. Emit only the per-file profile summary — do not
write any object YAML in this task.
evals.expected_output: A per-column profile of inventory.csv naming distinct-value counts (4 names, 2 roles, 2 statuses, 2 manufacturers, 2 port counts, 3 dates), sample values, length ranges, the 100% numeric port_count column, and the YYYY-MM-DD commissioned_at column as DateTime.
expectations: The profile names per-column distinct-value counts
role and status are flagged as low-cardinality (dropdown candidates)
port_count is flagged as 100% numeric
commissioned_at is flagged as a YYYY-MM-DD DateTime pattern
manufacturer_name is flagged as a reference candidate (low cardinality)

evals.prompt:
After running `infrahubctl object validate ./output_dir/ --branch csv-import-20260622-1430`,
the server returned:

    output_dir/04_devices.yml row 7 attribute 'status':
      'Active' is not a valid choice for status

The skill produced the YAML from `inventory.csv` row 12. The skill
knows the per-row mapping back to the source. Translate the
validate error into a clear instruction that tells me which CSV
cell to fix, and explicitly tells me NOT to edit the emitted YAML
directly.
evals.expected_output: A traceback that names inventory.csv row 12 column "Status", explains that "Active" is the display label (the schema choice name is `active`), and lists fix options including re-running with the dropdown-label-to-name rule applied. The response must instruct the user not to edit the emitted YAML by hand.
expectations: The error is traced back to inventory.csv row 12 column "Status"
The "Active" vs "active" label/name distinction is explained
Fix options include editing the CSV and re-running the skill
The user is told NOT to edit the emitted YAML directly

evals.prompt:
After emitting Infrahub object YAML to ./output_dir/ (do this
step first for the simple manufacturers CSV below), describe the
local self-check you would run BEFORE creating any branch. Name the
specific managing-objects rules you check the emission against and
what each one catches.

    # manufacturers.csv
    name,description,country
    Dell,Server and storage vendor,US

Schema defines `OrganizationManufacturer` with Text attributes
`name`, `description`, `country`, and `human_friendly_id: [name__value]`.
evals.expected_output: The emitted YAML for OrganizationManufacturer under ./output_dir/, plus a description of the local self-check that walks each emitted file against managing-objects rules (format-structure, value-attributes, value-relationships, children-components, range-expansion, organization-load-order). No infrahubctl command is run in this task — the self-check is local-only.
expectations: At least one YAML file is emitted to ./output_dir/
The self-check is described as running BEFORE infrahubctl branch create
The self-check is local-only (no server, no branch)
Specific managing-objects rule files are named
Each named rule is matched to the shape it verifies

evals.prompt:
Walk me through the validate-and-load sequence after the
local self-check passes for a hypothetical ./output_dir/. Include
the exact infrahubctl commands in the order they should be run,
using a default branch name like `csv-import-YYYYMMDD-HHMM`. Do
NOT actually execute them — just produce the command sequence
with one-line explanations.
evals.expected_output: The command sequence in order: (1) infrahubctl info to verify connectivity, (2) infrahubctl branch create <name>, (3) infrahubctl object validate ./output_dir/ --branch <name>, (4) infrahubctl object load ./output_dir/ --branch <name>. Plus a note about `infrahubctl branch delete <name>` on partial failure. The default branch is never the target.
expectations: The sequence creates a branch BEFORE validate or load
infrahubctl info is mentioned as a connectivity check
The branch is created via infrahubctl branch create
validate and load both pass --branch <name>
The default branch is explicitly excluded as a target
branch delete is named as the cleanup on partial failure

evals.prompt:
Before mapping `devices.csv` to the schema, describe how you
would discover the schema. List the priority chain (MCP →
infrahubctl schema export → /api/schema REST → local schemas/*.yml)
and explain when to fall back to the next source. The schema is
read-only throughout — do not propose any schema edit. Do not
emit any YAML in this task — describe the introspection only.
evals.expected_output: A description of the four-source priority chain (MCP first, infrahubctl schema export, /api/schema REST, local schemas/*.yml last), with the trade-off of each source (freshness vs. availability) and an explicit "schema is read-only — escalate to managing-schemas if a column has no home."
expectations: The priority chain lists MCP first
infrahubctl schema export is listed as the next fallback after MCP
/api/schema REST is named as the third option
Local schemas/*.yml is named as the last resort with a freshness caveat
The response explicitly says no schema edit will be proposed
Escalation to managing-schemas is named for the unmappable-column case

evals.prompt:
I will hand you any of three input shapes — a single CSV
file, a directory of CSVs, or an explicit list of paths. Describe
how the skill normalizes each shape into a flat file list before
profiling, and what happens to non-CSV/TSV files in a directory.
Do not emit any YAML in this task — describe the input
normalization only.
evals.expected_output: A description of the input normalization step: single file passes through, directory is recursively scanned for *.csv and *.tsv (case-insensitive), list of paths is expanded the same way. Non-CSV/TSV files in a directory are ignored or surface a fail-closed report depending on the rule.
expectations: Single file is accepted directly
Directory is recursively scanned for .csv and .tsv files
Mixed input lists are normalized to a flat file set
Case-insensitive extension matching is mentioned
The response references the fail-closed posture for unsupported file types

evals.prompt:
I have `devices.csv` with a `license_tier` column the schema does
not declare anywhere:

    name,role,status,license_tier
    spine-01,spine,active,gold
    leaf-01,leaf,active,silver
    leaf-02,leaf,active,gold

The DcimDevice schema declares `name`, `role`, and `status` only — there
is no `license_tier` attribute, relationship, or dropdown, and I have not
approved any schema change. This is non-interactive: you cannot ask me to
skip the column.

Follow the fail-closed rule. If any column has no schema home, stop and
write nothing to `./output_dir/` — do not emit a partial file that drops
the column. Report the unmapped column instead.
evals.expected_output: No object YAML in ./output_dir/ — the skill stops because `license_tier` has no schema home and emits a structured report naming the unmapped column and the kind it checked, pointing the user at infrahub-managing-schemas. No partial file that silently drops the column.
expectations: No Object-envelope file in ./output_dir/ carries any spec.data rows
A report names the unmapped license_tier column and the kind checked
The user is routed to infrahub-managing-schemas for the schema decision
No partial emission that drops the unmapped column

assertions.name: fail-closed
assertions.check: No Object document in the output dir emits spec.data rows for an input that has an unmapped column

evals.prompt:
I have a directory `./imports/` holding three CSV files, one kind
each:

    # imports/manufacturers.csv
    name,country
    Arista,US
    Cisco,US

    # imports/sites.csv
    name,country
    par-1,FR
    nyc-1,US

    # imports/devices.csv
    name,role,site,manufacturer
    spine-01,spine,par-1,Arista
    edge-01,edge,nyc-1,Cisco

Schema (fixed): OrganizationManufacturer (`human_friendly_id:
[name__value]`), LocationSite (`human_friendly_id: [name__value]`), and
DcimDevice (`human_friendly_id: [name__value]`, relationships `site` →
LocationSite and `manufacturer` → OrganizationManufacturer).

Treat the directory as the input: scan it, normalize to a flat file list,
and emit Infrahub object YAML for every file to `./output_dir/`, numbered
for load order.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_dir/ with NN-prefixed files covering all three input kinds — OrganizationManufacturer, LocationSite, and DcimDevice — with manufacturers and sites loading before devices, and scalar HFID references from devices to each.
expectations: All three input kinds appear in the emission (no file dropped)
Manufacturers and sites are numbered before devices
Files use NN_ load-order prefixes
Envelope is correct on every emitted doc

assertions.name: envelope
assertions.check: Every emitted YAML doc has apiVersion, kind: Object, spec.kind, and spec.data list

assertions.name: load-order-numbering
assertions.check: Emitted files use NN_ numeric prefixes for load order

assertions.name: folder-coverage
assertions.check: Every kind implied by the input directory appears in the emission; no file dropped during folder expansion
